commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bb4ed9544d2640fb466a317964c144d83f4b4b40)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an experimental deferred Gateway activation mode with a token-gated loopback control port, CLI env opt-in, activation-time config rechecks, supervised recovery changes, and Gateway/CLI tests.

PR surface: Source +515, Tests +881. Total +1396 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: deferred parking always calls listenGatewayHttpServer with bindHost set to 127.0.0.1 while current active startup resolves bind-specific listen hosts first.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer product and security-boundary approval is needed in addition to the bind-contract fix, so this should not be treated as an autonomous repair-only lane.

target.title: feat(gateway): add deferred activation control port
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Gateway startup currently has to do all Gateway process/module work at the moment the user actually needs the Gateway. Container orchestrators that can start a process before final user assignment have no safe upstream seam for parking a warmed Gateway process and activating it later with fresh config.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds an experimental, opt-in deferred Gateway activation seam:

- `startGatewayServer(..., { activationMode: "deferred" })` returns a parked Gateway handle.
- The parked process binds a Gateway-port placeholder that keeps `/health` and `/healthz` live while `/readyz` remains unavailable.
- A loopback activation control port exposes `GET /healthz`, `GET /readyz`, and token-gated `POST /activate`.
- `openclaw gateway run` opts in only when both `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_DEFERRED_ACTIVATION_CONTROL_PORT` and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_DEFERRED_ACTIVATION_TOKEN` are set.
- Activation re-reads and reapplies current config/env, reruns startup guards, refreshes managed proxy routing, revalidates bind/auth safety, rejects TLS configs (until a TLS-compatible placeholder exists), and starts the real Gateway in the already-warmed process.

The control path is intentionally conservative:

- token-gated and loopback-only
- activation token refresh honors env-over-config precedence and config-managed token revocation
- Gateway-port placeholder preserves supervised lock/liveness behavior
- supervised recovery probes both deferred control-port liveness and the existing Gateway port for upgrade compatibility
- close waits for in-flight real Gateway startup once it has begun, but stays bounded while still in pre-start validation

## User Impact

This does not change default Gateway startup behavior. It adds a generic upstream primitive for runtimes/orchestrators that want to prestart a parked Gateway process and activate it after final assignment.

Normal eager Gateway startup is effectively unchanged in the generic startup benchmark (noise-level deltas):

- base artifact: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/base-d239/gateway-startup.json`
- branch artifact: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/gateway-startup.json`

| case | base p50 ready ms | branch p50 ready ms | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| default | 3029.0 | 2981.0 | -48.0ms (-1.6%) |
| skipChannels | 2973.9 | 2974.7 | +0.7ms (+0.0%) |
| oneInternalHook | 2988.9 | 2987.2 | -1.7ms (-0.1%) |
| allInternalHooks | 2971.4 | 2970.7 | -0.6ms (-0.0%) |
| fiftyPlugins | 3305.4 | 3374.3 | +69.0ms (+2.1%) |
| fiftyStartupLazyPlugins | 3092.8 | 3009.8 | -83.1ms (-2.7%) |

Deferred CLI microbenchmark evidence on this branch:

- artifact dir: `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/cli-deferred/`
- eager spawn-to-ready p50: `3035.7ms`
- deferred parked-ready p50: `2744.8ms`
- deferred activation POST-to-ready p50 after a parked process exists: `353.0ms`
- deferred total spawn-to-active p50: `3099.7ms` (roughly flat vs eager, as expected; benefit is moving most startup before assignment)

## Evidence

Pinned base for this branch: `d23977edbcf945057eecd5cbd713489a4dd8ef16`.

Passed locally on rebased branch head `bb4ed9544d`:

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.deferred-activation.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.supervised-lock.test.ts`
  - gateway deferred activation tests: 32 passed across gateway shards
  - CLI option/supervised lock tests: 79 passed across CLI shards
- `OPENCLAW_TSGO_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm build:docker`
- `codex review --base d23977edbcf945057eecd5cbd713489a4dd8ef16`
  - result: `No discrete, high-confidence regressions were identified in the changed Gateway deferred-activation and CLI startup paths.`
- `pnpm test:startup:gateway --runs 5 --warmup 1 --output .artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/gateway-startup.json`
- CLI deferred/eager microbenchmark using `.artifacts/deferred-activation/bench-cli-deferred.mjs` with token-gated activation control port; outputs in `.artifacts/deferred-activation/rebased-final/cli-deferred/`.
